sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.

You are still on the same level.

Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'MOUSE_L2': 'third blue (row 36-39, col 1) moves charcoal LEFT by 4 cols; bottom blue (row 44-47, col 1) moves charcoal RIGHT by 4 cols. Top 2 blues have no effect on charcoal.'}
- Object taxonomy: {'P': 'pink top bar (progress/decreasing counter)', 'G': 'dark gray background', 'W': 'white background regions', 'b': 'blue squares - movement buttons (4 on left edge)', 'B': 'black bars (horizontal obstacles)', 'N': 'light green strips (move with charcoal)', 'c': 'charcoal square (movable target object)'}
- HUD map: {'row_0': 'pink progress bar, decreasing with each action'}
- Win pattern: {'level_1': 'Move charcoal left 1 time to cross from white into dark gray region'}
- Level log: {'level_1': '1 left move completed level (charcoal crossed region boundary)'}
- Cross-level notes: Goal pattern: move charcoal across a region boundary. Each level has different layout and required direction.
Current level:
- World model: Charcoal at cols 6-9, white region at cols 0-3, green strip at cols 4-5. No overlap yet. Charcoal needs to move left more to enter white region. White region shrinks as charcoal approaches.
- Goal model: Move charcoal so it overlaps with the white region at bottom-left
- Recent findings: ['Charcoal moves left with third blue (row 37, col 1), 4 cols per click', 'White region shrinks as charcoal moves left (was cols 0-7, now 0-3)', 'Green strip moves with charcoal', 'Pink bar decreases with each action (currently px=49)']
- Open questions: ['Does charcoal need to fully enter white region, or just touch it?', 'Will white region continue shrinking, preventing overlap?']
- Plan: Continue moving charcoal left with third blue until it overlaps white region or level completes
- Failed probes: ['MOUSE(row=17,col=1) top blue: no charcoal movement', 'MOUSE(row=25,col=1) second blue: no charcoal movement', 'MOUSE(row=45,col=1) bottom blue: moves right but charcoal stopped at cols 22-25']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 35 actions; a strong score needs about 18 or fewer. You are 1.9x over the target.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking at col 3 within the blue region still only affected the top charcoal (moved from col 20 to col 19, px 44→45). The bottom charcoal didn't move at all.
So all clicks within the blue region (rows 24-39, cols 0-3) affect only the top charcoal. The bottom charcoal hasn't moved with any click I've tried.
Let me reconsider the problem. Maybe the bottom charcoal is NOT meant to be moved. Maybe it's already in the correct position, and the goal is only about the top charcoal.
Looking at the top charcoal: it's at [0,19]-[0,63], px=45. The white region below is at rows 1-19, cols 0-51.
The charcoal's left edge is at col 19, and the white region's right edge is at col 51. The charcoal extends from col 19 to col 63, so it overlaps with the white region from col 19 to col 51.
Wait, let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is for the top charcoal to be the SAME WIDTH as the white region. The white region is 52 cols wide (cols 0-51), and the charcoal is 45 cols wide. If I click 7 more times, the charcoal would be 52 cols wide (matching the white region).
Or maybe the goal is for the charcoal to reach the LEFT edge of the white region (col 0). Currently at col 19, so 19 more clicks.
Or maybe the goal is for the charcoal to be CENTERED relative to the white region. The white region is cols 0-51 (center at col 25.5), and the charcoal is cols 19-63 (center at col 41). If centered, the charcoal would be at cols 4-55 (center at col 29.5), which is close to the white region's center.
Actually, let me think about what "crossing a region boundary" means in the context of level 1. In level 1, the charcoal was moved left to cross from the white region into the dark gray region. For level 2, maybe the goal is similar: move the top charcoal so it crosses from the white region into the dark gray region on the LEFT side.
Currently the charcoal is at cols 19-63. The white region is at cols 0-51. The charcoal's left edge (col 19) is within the white region. If I move it left by 19, the left edge would be at col 0, which is the left edge of the white region. Moving it one more would put the left edge at col -1 (off the board), which doesn't make sense.
Wait, maybe the goal is for the charcoal to reach col 0 (the left edge of the board/white region). Currently at col 19, so 19 more clicks. But that's a lot of actions.
Let me try a different approach. Let me check if the white regions change when the charcoal reaches a certain position. Earlier, the white regions changed once (after the second blue click), and then stayed the same. Maybe the white regions change when the charcoal reaches a certain alignment.
Actually, let me look at the pink bar. It's decreasing with each action. Currently it's 20 chars wide (from the ASCII: "PPPPPPPPPPPPPPPPPP"). If it reaches 0, maybe the level is complete (or fails).
Let me count the pink bar width: "PPPPPPPPPPPPPPPPPP" = 20 P's. So the pink bar is 20 chars wide.
Hmm, I'm running out of ideas. Let me try clicking on the bottom charcoal directly to see if it moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking col 3 still only affects top charcoal. Bottom charcoal never moves.
# Let me try clicking on the bottom charcoal directly
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 3}])

# Check what changed
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
for r in [0, 52, 54]:
    print(f"Row {r}: {lines[r][:64]}")
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c':
        b = n['boundary']
        r1,c1 = min(p[0] for p in b), min(p[1] for p in b)
        r2,c2 = max(p[0] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Charcoal: [{r1},{c1}]-[{r2},{c2}], px={n['pixels']}")
print("done:", last_action_result.get('done'), "level_completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

Bottom charcoal [52,2]-[57,5], px=16. All blue clicks only affect top charcoal.", "goal_model": "Maybe only top charcoal matters? Need to reach col 0 or match white width?", "recent_findings": ["Col within blue region doesn't matter, all clicks affect top charcoal"], "open_questions": ["How to move bottom charcoal? What is exact win condition?"], "current_plan": "Try clicking on bottom charcoal directly", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["Click at col 3 within blue: same effect as col 1"]}}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
